learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate understanding of how creative content behaves in different media contexts and adapts to different understandings of user engagement.
Collaborate effectively in creative teams across the full semester, with documented individual contribution to group concept development.
Produce original media assets that showcase a transmedia storyworld, drawing on practices such as photography, graphic design, illustration, and audio-visual production.
Critically justify creative decisions, including reflection on authentic authorship, applying transmedia concepts and relevant theoretical ideas covered in the course.

assessments.due_date:
Week 5 - Week 12

The due date for your AI Charter (A1 Part A) and Portfolio (A1 Part B) is BEFORE your tutorial when your consultation is due.
assessments.task_description:
This assessment involves two consultations with your tutor, scheduled during tutorial time: 

  • Assessment 1 Part A = 15%
  • Assessment 1 Part B = 20%

These consultations are centred around the students’ media portfolio work. Students will explain and justify their creative and technical decisions, demonstrating critical analysis skills developed in the course, by using concepts and vocabulary pertinent to transmedia storytelling. 

Assessment 1 Part A (15%)

Assessment 1 Part A has 2 components: AI Charter and Portfolio Consultation.  

AI CHARTER: As a team you will write an AI Charter and submit it individually to Turnitin. The AI Charter is a written team agreement that establishes how your group will and will not use artificial intelligence tools throughout this project. It should address: 

  1. Authorship: Where does your creative contribution end and AI assistance begin? 
  2. Use agreements: Which tools, if any, will you use, and for what specific tasks? What will you not use AI for? 

The charter will be revisited in Assessment 3, where you will reflect on how your actual AI use compared to your original commitments. 

PORTFOLIO CONSULTATION: As a team, you will describe what your transmedia project is. Individually, each team member will explain: 

  1. What is your unique contribution to your team’s transmedia project 
  2. What have you done so far, what are your plans for Assessment 2 submission and how you plan to explore your medium’s strengths.  

Marking split: Team components (AI charter + project concept question) are marked as a team (50%); individual responses receive individual marks (50%).   

Conditions:  

  • Portfolio consultations happen at students' tables: this is not a formal presentation. 
  • All materials (phones, notebooks) should be packed away before the consultation begins. 
  • Teams may use one slide on a laptop or one printed visual aid. If a laptop is used, the screen should be visible to all team members at all times. 
  • Visual aids are support tools only and are not assessed.  
  • No notes or machine translation tools may be used during the consultation.  

Assessment 1 Part B (20%)

The second portfolio consultation is entirely individual and will happen on week 11 or 12. A schedule will be made available on Blackboard. During this consultation, you will explain how you are responding to the feedback you received on Assessment 2, and how that response is shaping your Assessment 3 submission.  

Conditions:  

  • BEFORE your consultation, you must submit at least one media asset to Blackboard that demonstrates how you are addressing your Assessment 2 feedback. You may refer to this submission during the consultation.  
  • If you have not submitted anything to Blackboard by the time your tutor calls you for your consultation, you will not be permitted to sit the consultation. 
  • No notes or machine translation tools may be used during the consultation. 

Full details and a comprehensive grading rubric are provided on Blackboard under "Assessments".
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assignments for this course will be submitted electronically via TurnItIn (A1 Part A) and Blackboard (A1 Part B). Before submitting any assignments for this course, you must ensure you have completed UQ's compulsory online Academic Integrity Tutorial.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
You will work in a creative team (4 students) to create a transmedia storyworld that exists authentically across multiple platforms. Each team member is responsible for one platform/medium, ensuring that the story elements respond and capitalise on each medium’s unique features (medium specificity).   

Assessment components (submitted individually on Blackboard):   

  1. Media portfolio (work-in-progress stage) 
  2. Process Evidence 
  3. Media Strategy Statement 


1 Media Portfolio: Each person in the team must produce a media portfolio of creative and original work using the Adobe Suite flagship programs (Photoshop, Premiere Pro, Audition, Illustrator, InDesign). We want you to use these software because they are the industry standard. At this point, we are interested in work-in-progress stage of your media portfolio. The final version will be submitted in Assessment 3.  

You can use a variety of different formats, such as drawing and illustration, still photography, posters, audio-visual material, including video and podcast, social media account, game prototype, among others. We are open to your ideas!  

Examples of adequately substantial individual media portfolio submitted at the mid-semester stage will be discussed in tutorials and lectures.  

Original content only:   

  • No stock images or footage (except scoring/music).   
  • Only content produced during the period of this course (no old pictures/footage).  


2 Process Evidence: You must provide clear evidence of your creative and technical process. This evidence should allow markers to verify that you have used the Adobe Creative suite and that you are using your own images. You process evidence document should demonstrate:

  1. How you created your media portfolio.
  2. How the Adobe Suite was used. 
  3. Did you use AI or Machine Translation tools at any stage? If yes, specify which tools, how, and why, and what parts of the work are fully your own and what parts are AI generated. Was it helpful? How? Justify the use of these tools.   

Examples of evidence includes, but are not limited to:  

  • Screenshots of Adobe timelines/layers  
  • Drafts, early exports, failed tests  
  • Time lapse of your drawing / editing  
  • Original assets files  
  • Version history  


3 Media Strategy Statement: You must submit a written statement (500 words) addressing the following questions:  

  1. What is your team storyworld about? How is your media output contributing to your team's storyworld? 
  2. How are you capitalising on medium specificity?  
  3. How have the course’s ideas informed your work? 


Full details and a comprehensive grading rubric are provided on Blackboard under "Assessments".
assessments.submission_guidelines: Portfolio and Statement: Submitted via Blackboard. Large files can be submitted as links to online folders (like OneDrive) or online platforms (like YouTube) - in this case, the student must ensure that the link is accessible to the marker. Each person is responsible for submitting their own materials that they want marked on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The final project is your final media portfolio, ready for publication, that should incorporate feedback from previous assessments.  

Assessment 3 components (submitted individually on Blackboard):  

  1. Media portfolio (Final version, ready to be published).   
  2. Process Evidence 
  3. Media Strategy Statement + AI Charter update
  4. Peer Assessment Statement   


1 Media Portfolio: This is your final work, ready to released into the world. At this stage, the individual portfolio should demonstrate noticeable improvement from the Assessment 2, including new media developed since the midterm, pushing your storyworld concept further. You are expected to work with the Adobe Suite flagship programs (Photoshop, Premiere Pro, Audition, Illustrator, InDesign) in the production of your portfolio.   

Examples of adequately substantial individual final media portfolios will be discussed in tutorials and lectures.  

Original content only:   

  • No stock images or footage (except scoring/music).   
  • Only content produced during the period of this course (no old pictures/footage). 


2 Process Evidence: You must provide clear evidence of your creative and technical process. This evidence should allow markers to verify that you have used the Adobe Creative suite and that you are using your own images. You process evidence document should demonstrate: 

  1. How you created your media portfolio. 
  2. How the Adobe Suite was used.   
  3. Did you use AI or Machine Translation tools at any stage? If yes, specify which tools, how, and why, and what parts of the work are fully your own and what parts are AI generated. Was it helpful? How? Justify the use of these tools.     

Examples of evidence includes, but are not limited to:   

  • Screenshots of Adobe timelines/layers 
  • Drafts, early exports, failed tests   
  • Time lapse of your drawing / editing   
  • Original assets files   
  • Version history  


3 Media Strategy Statement + AI Charter Update: Your media strategy statement (700 words) should explain: 

  1. What did you make and how is this portfolio part of a transmedia project?  
  2. How does your media output foster active participation?  
  3. How did the AI Charter work for you and your team? Would you review/modify the charter? How?  


4 Peer Assessment Statement: A statement (300 words) addressing the group dynamics:  

  1. How did you work with your team to accomplish this task? And did this approach work well?  
  2. Were there any issues with collaboration, and if so, how were they managed?  
  3. What challenges did you face, and what might you do differently next time?


Full details and a comprehensive grading rubric are provided on Blackboard under "Assessments".

Large files can be submitted as links to online folders (like OneDrive) or online platforms (like YouTube) - in this case, the student must ensure that the link is accessible to the marker. Each person is responsible for submitting their own materials that they want marked on Blackboard.
